name: Brachycephalic Obstructive Airway Syndrome (BOAS)
description: BOAS is a complex of anatomical abnormalities in flat-faced breeds including stenotic nares, elongated soft palate, hypoplastic trachea, and everted laryngeal saccules. These combine to severely restrict airflow and cause chronic respiratory distress.
treatment_overview: Surgical correction of stenotic nares, elongated soft palate, and everted saccules significantly improves quality of life. Weight control and avoiding heat/exercise are essential management measures.
prevention_tips: Select breeding stock with wider nostrils and better airway scores
Use BOAS functional grading schemes before breeding
Avoid breeding from severely affected dogs
